subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **108 cards** total:
  - **14** Tempura (×2 for a set)
  - **14** Sashimi (×3 for a set)
  - **14** Dumpling (1-5+ sets)
  - **12** Maki Rolls (1, 2, or 3 roll icons)
  - **10** Salmon Nigiri
  - **5** Squid Nigiri
  - **5** Egg Nigiri
  - **10** Pudding
  - **6** Wasabi
  - **4** Chopsticks

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle all 108 cards.**
2. **Deal cards** based on player count:
   - 2 players: 10 cards each
   - 3 players: 9 cards each
   - 4 players: 8 cards each
   - 5 players: 7 cards each
3. **Keep remaining cards** as draw pile for future rounds.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Hold hand** of dealt cards.
- **Keep score** with pen and paper or the score tracker.
- **3 rounds** per game with fresh deals.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Sushi Go! is a fast-paced card-drafting game. Pick cards from a hand, pass the rest, and try to collect the best combinations of sushi dishes for points over 3 rounds.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
All players act simultaneously:
1. **Choose 1 card** from your hand and place it face down.
2. **Reveal** — All players flip their chosen card face up.
3. **Pass remaining hand** to the player on your left.
4. **Repeat** until all cards are played.
5. **Score the round** — Calculate points for all collected cards.
6. **Discard** all cards except Pudding (kept until game end).
7. **Deal new hands** for rounds 2 and 3.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Card Types & Scoring
- **Maki Rolls** — Player with most maki roll icons: 6 pts. Second most: 3 pts.
- **Tempura** — Set of 2: 5 pts. Single: 0 pts.
- **Sashimi** — Set of 3: 10 pts. Fewer: 0 pts.
- **Dumplings** — 1: 1 pt, 2: 3 pts, 3: 6 pts, 4: 10 pts, 5+: 15 pts.
- **Nigiri** — Egg: 1 pt, Salmon: 2 pts, Squid: 3 pts.
- **Wasabi** — Triples the value of the next Nigiri played on it.
- **Pudding** — Scored at game end. Most pudding: 6 pts. Fewest: −6 pts.
- **Chopsticks** — On a future turn, swap Chopsticks back into the passing hand to play 2 cards instead of 1.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Chopsticks timing** — Say "Sushi Go!" when using chopsticks to take 2 cards.
- **Maki ties** — Split points evenly (round down) among tied players.
- **Pudding ties** — Split end-game bonus/penalty among tied players.
- **2-player pudding** — In 2-player games, no penalty for fewest pudding.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Round Scoring
- Score all card types except pudding at end of each round.

#### Game End (After Round 3)
- Score pudding: most = 6 pts, fewest = −6 pts.
- Add all 3 round scores + pudding.
- **Highest total wins.** Ties: player with most pudding wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Complete sets** — A single Tempura or Sashimi is worth 0. Focus on completing them.
- **Dumplings scale up** — 5 dumplings = 15 points; they're powerful in quantity.
- **Wasabi + Squid Nigiri** — The best single combo (9 points).
- **Don't ignore pudding** — The −6 penalty at game end is brutal.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Read the draft** — Watch what's passed to you to infer what opponents collect.
- **Hate-draft** — Sometimes take a card to deny an opponent a set completion.
- **Maki timing** — Maki rolls are a reliable 6 points if you can lead the count.
- **Chopsticks early** — Take Chopsticks early so you can double-pick later.
- **Balance short and long term** — Score rounds well but don't ignore pudding.
